title: Crotalaria dilloniana
intro: Crotalaria dilloniana is a species of flowering plant in the pea family (Fabaceae), native to tropical Africa. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. Crotalaria dilloniana contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, including the isoquinoline alkaloids crotalinine and dillonianine, which are responsible for its medicinal activities. The plant is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
taxonomy: Crotalaria dilloniana is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It belongs to the genus Crotalaria and is native to tropical Africa. The classification of Crotalaria dilloniana includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Magnoliophyta, and class as Magnoliopsida. It is further classified as order Fabales, family Fabaceae, and tribe Crotalarieae. Within this classification, the species is known to be a member of the subgenus Crotalaria.
uses: Crotalaria dilloniana is a plant with potential medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness. It has been studied for its possible antimalarial and antibacterial activities. However, its traditional uses and exact medicinal applications are not well-documented in scientific literature. Further research is necessary to fully explore the medicinal uses of Crotalaria dilloniana.

side_effects: Crotalaria dilloniana contains pyrrolizidine alkaloids, which can be toxic to humans and animals if ingested. Ingestion of the plant may lead to liver damage, cirrhosis, and other health problems. If Crotalaria dilloniana is handled extensively, skin and eye irritation may occur due to the presence of alkaloids. Prolonged exposure to the plant's alkaloids can cause severe health issues, including liver failure. It is essential to exercise caution and avoid contact with Crotalaria dilloniana to prevent potential side effects.
